text.title: aws-multi-account-landing-zone Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for aws-multi-account-landing-zone. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: The management account MUST contain no workloads. It is used only for AWS Organizations, consolidated billing, and IAM Identity Center. All application workloads MUST live in member accounts under the Workloads OU. The management account MUST NOT have access keys for human users — only federated access via IAM Identity Center.
rule.rationale: The management account has elevated trust across the organization. A compromised management account can modify SCPs and create new accounts. Keeping it workload-free minimizes the attack surface and satisfies compliance requirements that mandate management plane separation.

rule.statement: SCPs MUST be applied at the OU level, not at individual accounts. The OU structure MUST include at minimum: Security OU (log-archive account, security-tooling account), Infrastructure OU (network account, shared-services account), Workloads OU (nested Prod and Non-Prod OUs), Sandbox OU, and Suspended OU for decommissioned accounts.
rule.rationale: OU-level SCPs ensure that every account created under that OU inherits the policy automatically. Account-level SCP attachments require manual maintenance and are easily missed when provisioning new accounts via Account Factory.
